cot_content:
Okay, so I need to figure out how to separate egg yolk from the white. The options are A or B. Let me think.

The question is about separating the yolk from the white. I remember that when you crack an egg, the yolk is usually surrounded by the white, and you need a method to get them apart. 

Option A says to pass the egg back and forth between hands with closed fingers. Option B says with spread fingers. Hmm. From what I've seen before, when you crack the egg into your hand, if you keep your fingers together (closed), the yolk tends to get stuck in the center while the white runs through your fingers. By moving the egg back and forth with closed fingers, the yolk stays intact and the white drips off. 

Alternatively, if your fingers are spread apart, maybe the yolk would fall through or not be contained as well. I think the key is to keep the fingers closed so the yolk remains in the middle and the white can be let go by opening your fingers slightly to let the white drain. 

I've also heard of using a spoon to catch the yolk, but that's not one of the options here. The method with your hands relies on the tension of the yolk membrane and the flow of the white. Closed fingers would help contain the yolk while allowing the white to pass through when you slightly open your fingers. 

So I think the correct answer is A.
label: minority
